response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

Reach for the benefits of supporting local artisans and workers. The customer will pay $170 for the handcrafted table and $127.50 for the factory-made chairs during the promotional week. The total amount paid by the customer will be $297.50. The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items, which means the customer saves $30 on the table and $11.25 on the chairs. The customer will pay $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs, totaling $297.50. The discount is applied to each item separately, ensuring that the customer pays the reduced price for each item. The promotional week is a great opportunity to support local factory workers and enjoy a better price on handmade products.
